2: Why: A standardized evaluation and monitoring framework provides a systematic way
3: to assess and ensure the ongoing performance of intelligent agents. This involves
4: defining clear metrics for accuracy, latency, and resource consumption, like token
5: usage for LLMs. It also includes advanced techniques such as analyzing agentic
6: trajectories to understand the reasoning process and employing an LLM-as-a-Judge
7: for nuanced, qualitative assessments. By establishing feedback loops and reporting
8: systems, this framework allows for continuous improvement, A/B testing, and the
9: detection of anomalies or performance drift, ensuring the agent remains aligned with
11: Rule of thumb: Use this pattern when deploying agents in live, production
12: environments where real-time performance and reliability are critical. Additionally, use
13: it when needing to systematically compare different versions of an agent or its
14: underlying models to drive improvements, and when operating in regulated or
15: high-stakes domains requiring compliance, safety, and ethical audits. This pattern is
16: also suitable when an agent's performance may degrade over time due to changes in
17: data or the environment (drift), or when evaluating complex agentic behavior,
18: including the sequence of actions (trajectory) and the quality of subjective outputs
